bio:
I am a Los Angeles-born writer and event producer deeply interested in the human condition.

My writing has lead me to farmland of California to smoke weed with nuns, New Mexico to eat green chili hash with those making a difference, play at a chess club in New York City, and talk about rockstars with the world's first self proclaimed groupie.

I am the co-founder of Club Chess. A party that can best be described by The New York Times, The Guardian, x, and x.

I was the editorial producer for Zora Zine, a magazine that looked towards the future of blockchain technology and cultural discourse that surrounds it. I am most proud of the print book I worked on, published by Actual Source.

I have given a talk on The Metaphysics of Party Culture.

I am currently the writer and head of events at Zora.

I would like to go to more places and speak to more people.

I believe in: properly curating a room, the skill of interviewing, writing well, and an ice cold martini (vodka).

I am most interested in seeing what I can get away with.
